source_docs.title: VASA-seq Supplementary Table 14: reagents and equipment
VASA-seq Supplementary Table 12: oligos, probes, and dual-index primers

oligos.name: CEL-seq2/SORT-seq barcoded oligo-dT RT primer
oligos.role: Barcoded reverse-transcription primer pre-dispensed into each 384-well; carries the T7 promoter, the Illumina small-RNA Read 1 region, a 6-nt UFI/UMI, an 8-nt well-specific cell barcode and an anchored poly(dT) that primes first-strand cDNA on the poly(A)-tailed fragments

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter for IVT linear amplification

components.name: Illumina small-RNA Read 1 region
components.role: 5' Illumina (RA5-derived) adapter / Read 1 primer binding region

components.name: UFI / UMI
components.role: 6-nt unique fragment identifier / unique molecular identifier

components.name: Cell barcode
components.role: 8-nt well-specific cell barcode (384 barcodes, one per 384-well; sequences in GSE176588)

components.name: Poly(dT)
components.role: anchored oligo-dT that captures the poly(A) tail added to each RNA fragment

oligos.notes: Cel-seq2 primers for VASA-plate = Muraro et al., 2016 (SORT-seq); the exact per-well 8-nt barcodes are deposited at GSE176588 and are not printed in these documents. Constant T7-promoter + Illumina-small-RNA portions are the canonical CEL-seq2/SORT-seq design (Hashimshony 2016); the doc gives order as 6-nt UFI/UMI then 8-nt cell barcode. Poly(dT) length shown (24 T) is the CEL-seq2 standard, not explicitly stated in the doc. Used at 0.25 uM, 50 nl/well.

oligos.name: RA3 ligation oligonucleotide (3' adapter)
oligos.role: Pre-adenylated, 3'-blocked RNA/DNA adapter ligated by T4 RNA Ligase 2 (truncated) to the 3' end of the rRNA-depleted aRNA, providing the RTP priming site for the second reverse transcription

components.name: 5' App
components.role: 5' adenylation for ligase-independent (T4 RNL2 truncated) ligation

components.name: 3' C3 spacer
components.role: 3' blocking group to prevent self/concatemer ligation

oligos.notes: RA3 oligonucleotide for VASA-plate = Hashimshony et al., 2012 (standard Illumina RA3). Sequence transcribed from the RA3-for-VASA-drop entry (this study), which is the identical standard RA3; used at 20 uM. Added at 25 C for 1 h.

oligos.name: RTP oligonucleotide (reverse-transcription primer)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer complementary to the ligated RA3 adapter; primes second-strand cDNA synthesis (SuperScript III) on the adapter-ligated aRNA

components.name: RTP primer
components.role: anneals to the RA3 adapter (reverse complement of TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGC)

oligos.notes: RTP oligonucleotide for VASA-plate = Hashimshony et al., 2012 (standard Illumina RTP). Sequence transcribed from the RTP-for-VASA-drop entry, which is the identical standard RTP; used at 20 uM.

oligos.name: PE1 library PCR primer (P5 / RP1)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer that adds the P5 flow-cell adapter and completes the Illumina small-RNA Read 1 region on the barcode/cell-barcode side of the library

components.name: Illumina small-RNA Read 1
components.role: anneals to the Read 1 / GTTCTACAGTCCGA region introduced by the CEL-seq2 primer

oligos.notes: PE1/PE2 primer mix (5 uM each) for VASA-plate = the CEL-seq2/SORT-seq PE primers (Hashimshony 2012 / Muraro 2016). Exact sequence not printed in these documents; shown here as the canonical Illumina RP1/small-RNA P5 primer. P5 constant matches verified P5.

oligos.name: PE2 library PCR primer (P7, indexed / RPI)
oligos.role: Indexed library PCR primer that adds the sample (i7) index and the P7 flow-cell adapter; anneals to the RA3/RTP region on the cDNA-insert side of the library

components.name: i7 sample index
components.role: 8-nt sample/plate index (read as the 8-cycle index read on NextSeq)

components.name: RA3/RTP anneal region
components.role: GTGACTGGAGTTC linker + reverse complement of RA3; anneals to the RTP/RA3 region

oligos.notes: CEL-seq2/SORT-seq indexed P7 PCR primer (Muraro 2016 / Illumina RPIX small-RNA design). Exact sequence not printed in these documents; index shown as an 8-nt token because the VASA-plate NextSeq run uses an 8-cycle index read. P7 constant matches verified P7.

oligos.name: Illumina small-RNA Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer; anneals to the P5-side small-RNA read region and reads the 6-nt UMI followed by the 8-nt cell barcode
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina small-RNA Read 1 primer; its sequence is built into the library on the P5 side by the CEL-seq2 primer + PE1. Read 1 = 26 cycles (reads UMI[6] + cell barcode[8], then poly(dT) which is trimmed).

oligos.name: Read 2 sequencing primer (RA3/RTP side)
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer; anneals to the RA3/RTP-derived region on the P7 side and reads the cDNA insert across the gene body

components.name: Read 2 primer
components.role: anneals to the RA3/RTP region and reads into the cDNA insert

oligos.notes: Read 2 (135 cycles) reads the cDNA insert. Exact custom Read 2 primer sequence for VASA-plate/SORT-seq is not printed in these documents; represented here by the RA3/RTP-region sequence it anneals to. Best-effort per skill guidance.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: P5 flow-cell binding adapter (5' end of the final library)
oligos.notes: Introduced by the PE1 (P5) library PCR primer.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: P7 flow-cell binding adapter (3' end of the final library)
oligos.notes: Introduced by the PE2 (P7, indexed) library PCR primer; appears as its reverse complement (ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG) at the 3' end of the top strand.

oligos.name: rRNA depletion probes (antisense to aRNA)
oligos.role: Pool of DNA probes complementary to amplified ribosomal aRNA (18S, 28S, 5.8S, 16S/12S mito, 5S); hybridize to rRNA-derived aRNA so it can be cleaved by thermostable RNaseH and removed
oligos.notes: Full probe set is Table S12 (this study); the probes used against the aRNA are the reverse complement of published probes (Adiconis et al., 2013). ~195 probes total (50-mers) tiling 18S/28S/5.8S/5S nuclear and 16S/12S mitochondrial rRNA. One representative sequence per rRNA class is shown; hybridized at 25 uM, cleaved with Thermostable RNaseH (Epicentre).

final_library.source_label: VASA-plate (VASA-seq, Salmen & De Jonghe et al., Nat Biotechnol 2022; CEL-seq2/SORT-seq small-RNA-style library)
annotation_lines: GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC = Illumina small-RNA Read 1 primer region
TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT = poly(dT) (oligo-dT capture of poly(A) tail)

evidence.locator: VASA-plate (VASA-seq, Salmen & De Jonghe et al., Nat Biotechnol 2022; CEL-seq2/SORT-seq small-RNA-style library)

library_generation.title: Cell sorting into barcoded 384-well plates
library_generation.summary: Single cells are FACS-sorted into 384-well plates, each well pre-filled with mineral oil and a well-specific barcoded CEL-seq2/SORT-seq oligo-dT primer.
library_generation.note: A BD FACSJazz sorts one cell per well into 5 ul mineral oil + 50 nl of 0.25 uM CEL-seq2/SORT-seq primer. Each well's primer carries a distinct 8-nt cell barcode, so all downstream molecules from that cell are tagged by well identity.
library_generation.product:
(1) Well contents before lysis (primer only):

5'- GCCGGT[T7 promoter TAATACGACTCACTATAGGG]AGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC[UMI:6][CELL_BARCODE:8]TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT -3'

library_generation.title: Cell lysis and RNA fragmentation
library_generation.summary: Cells are lysed with Thermolabile Proteinase K + IGEPAL, then all RNA is heat-fragmented at 85 C.
library_generation.note: Lysis 25 C 1 h then 55 C 10 min; Proteinase K heat-inactivated; RNA fragmented by heating to 85 C for 3 min. Fragmentation is what lets VASA capture the whole transcriptome (including non-polyadenylated RNA), not just transcript termini.
library_generation.product:
(2) Fragmented total RNA (all biotypes), free 3'-OH ends:

5'- [RNA fragment] -3'   (many fragments per cell; poly(A)+ and poly(A)- )

library_generation.title: RNA end-repair and poly(A) tailing
library_generation.summary: T4 PNK repairs fragment ends and E. coli poly(A) polymerase adds a poly(A) tail to every fragment so it can be primed by the oligo-dT.
library_generation.note: T4 polynucleotide kinase repairs 5'/3' ends; E. coli poly(A) polymerase + ATP appends a 3' poly(A) tail to each RNA fragment. This universal dA-tailing (the 'A' in VASA) makes non-polyadenylated RNA capturable by the barcoded oligo-dT.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (barcoded oligo-dT)
library_generation.summary: SuperScript III extends the well-barcoded oligo-dT primer on the poly(A)-tailed fragments, making first-strand cDNA tagged with the UFI/UMI and cell barcode.
library_generation.note: The CEL-seq2 primer's poly(dT) anneals to the new poly(A) tail; RT (50 C, 1 h) copies the fragment, embedding the T7 promoter, Read 1 region, 6-nt UFI/UMI and 8-nt cell barcode at the 5' end of the cDNA.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: E. coli DNA Polymerase I + RNaseH convert the first-strand cDNA into double-stranded DNA carrying a functional T7 promoter.
library_generation.note: RNaseH nicks the template RNA; DNA Pol I performs nick-translation to synthesize the second strand (16 C 2 h, 85 C 20 min), yielding blunt dsDNA with the double-stranded T7 promoter needed for IVT.

library_generation.title: Pooling and in-vitro transcription (IVT)
library_generation.summary: Barcoded dsDNA from all wells is pooled and linearly amplified by T7 in-vitro transcription into antisense amplified RNA (aRNA).
library_generation.note: T7 RNA polymerase transcribes from the promoter, producing many antisense aRNA copies per molecule (linear, low-bias amplification, as in CEL-seq). aRNA carries the Read 1 region, UMI and cell barcode near its 5' end and the gene sequence toward its 3' end.

library_generation.title: rRNA depletion
library_generation.summary: Antisense probes tiling rRNA hybridize to ribosomal aRNA, which is then cleaved by thermostable RNaseH and DNase-treated.
library_generation.note: The aRNA is hybridized to a probe set (reverse complement of Adiconis 2013 probes, Table S12) covering 18S/28S/5.8S/5S and mitochondrial 12S/16S rRNA; Thermostable RNaseH degrades the DNA:RNA hybrids and RQ1 DNase removes the probes, leaving non-rRNA aRNA.

library_generation.title: RA3 3' adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: T4 RNA Ligase 2 (truncated) ligates the pre-adenylated, 3'-blocked RA3 adapter to the 3' end of the aRNA.
library_generation.note: The 5'-App / 3'-C3 RA3 adapter is ligated (25 C, 1 h) onto the free 3'-OH of each aRNA fragment, installing the priming site for the next reverse transcription (as in the CEL-seq/small-RNA workflow).

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription with RTP primer
library_generation.summary: The RTP primer anneals to the ligated RA3 adapter and SuperScript III copies the aRNA back into cDNA.
library_generation.note: RTP (GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA) primes on RA3; RT (50 C, 1 h) generates cDNA spanning RA3 -> gene -> cell barcode -> UMI -> Read 1 region. RNaseA then removes residual RNA before PCR.

library_generation.title: Indexed library PCR (PE1/PE2)
library_generation.summary: PCR with PE1 (P5) and indexed PE2 (P7) primers adds the P5/P7 flow-cell adapters and the i7 sample index, producing the final sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: PE1 anneals to the Read 1 region and adds P5; PE2 anneals to the RA3/RTP region and adds the 8-nt i7 index + P7 (7-8 cycles, NEBNext Hi-Fi for VASA-plate). Size-selected by 0.8x AMPure and QC'd on a Bioanalyzer.

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6-nt UFI/UMI then the 8-nt well-specific cell barcode; the anchored poly(dT) that follows is homopolymer-trimmed. Primer is identical to the top-strand Read 1 region, so it anneals to the bottom strand and reads in the top-strand sense.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 — 26 bp: reads 6-nt UFI/UMI + 8-nt cell barcode (poly-dT tail is homopolymer-trimmed)

              Read 1 primer 5'-GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC----------------------->
  top    5'- ...GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC NNNNNN NNNNNNNN TTTTTTTTTTTT [cDNA]... -3'
  bottom 3'- ...CAAGTCTCAAGATGTCAGGCTGCTAG NNNNNN NNNNNNNN AAAAAAAAAAAA [cDNA]... -5'
                                        UFI(6) CB(8)  poly(dT)

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index sequencing primer (anneals to the RA3/RTP-P7 constant region)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-nt sample (i7) index added by the PE2/P7 PCR primer. Single-indexed on NextSeq 500.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 1 (i7) — 8 bp: reads the 8-nt sample index

         i7 primer 5'-...GGGTGCCAAGGGAACTCCAGTCAC------->
  top    5'- ...GGGTGCCAAGGGAACTCCAGTCAC NNNNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
  bottom 3'- ...CCCACGGTTCCCTTGAGGTCAGTG NNNNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                                     i7(8)              (P7')

library_sequencing.note: Reads the cDNA insert across the gene body from the RA3/P7 side (135 cycles gives near-full-length total-RNA coverage). Primer is complementary to the top-strand RA3 adapter, anneals to the top strand and reads leftward into the insert.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 — 135 bp: reads the cDNA insert across the gene body (from the RA3/P7 side)

  top    5'- ...[cDNA] TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGG GAACTCCAGTCAC... -3'
  bottom 3'- ...[cDNA] ACCTTAAGAGCCCACGGTTCC CTTGAGGTCAGTG... -5'
         <-------------ACCTTGAGGCCCACGGTTCC-5'  wait
              <-------------CCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA-5'  (Read 2 primer)

title: VASA-plate
description: VASA-plate is the plate-based format of VASA-seq (vast transcriptome analysis of single cells by dA-tailing), a total-RNA single-cell method that captures both polyadenylated and non-polyadenylated transcripts across their full length. Single cells are FACS-sorted into 384-well plates pre-loaded with a barcoded CEL-seq2/SORT-seq oligo-dT primer; after lysis, all RNA is fragmented, end-repaired and poly(A)-tailed so every fragment can be primed by the oligo-dT, tagging it with a 6-nt UFI/UMI and an 8-nt well-specific cell barcode. Barcoded cDNA is linearly amplified by in-vitro transcription (T7), rRNA-depleted, then converted to an Illumina small-RNA-style library by RA3 3' adapter ligation, RTP-primed reverse transcription and indexed PCR. Read 1 recovers the UMI + cell barcode, the index read recovers the sample (i7) index, and Read 2 reads the cDNA insert across the gene body.

reference.label: VASA-seq Supplementary Table 14: reagents and equipment

original_publication.title: High-throughput total RNA sequencing in single cells using VASA-seq

throughput.summary: Plate-based total-RNA scRNA-seq for smaller numbers of sorted/rare cells (384 cells per plate). Highly sensitive: ~9,480 +/- 1,252 genes/cell at 75,000 reads/cell and ~15,248 +/- 1,092 genes/cell at 750,000 reads/cell (HEK293T). Library-prep cost ~$0.98 USD per cell.
throughput.cells: 384 single cells per 384-well plate (one barcode per well); benchmarking used n=174-192 VASA-plate cells. The droplet format (VASA-drop) was used for the >30,000-cell mouse embryo atlas.
throughput.rna: Total RNA (poly(A)+ and poly(A)-): protein-coding, lncRNA and sncRNA; even full-length gene-body coverage; ~1.4% sncRNA; 44.1 +/- 10.1% unspliced reads. Molecules counted with 6-nt UFI/UMI (strand-specific).
throughput.dna: Not applicable (RNA assay; the amplified cDNA library is the sequenced product).

publication.statistical_model: Molecule counting by UFI (unique fragment identifier) with strand specificity; barcodes error-corrected by Hamming distance <=1 (plate). Downstream statistics: bimodal vs single Weibull mixture for histone-content cell-cycle classification; beta-distribution CDF-beta model for differential splicing-node inclusion (MicroExonator/Whippet); scVelo stochastic/dynamical models for RNA velocity; two-sided t-test for differential expression.

other.label: Key innovation
other.value: RNA fragmentation + universal end-repair/poly(A) tailing after lysis, enabling capture of non-polyadenylated as well as polyadenylated transcripts across their full length.

other.label: Data availability
other.value: GEO GSE176588 (VASA-seq mouse gastrulation/organogenesis atlas). Code: https://github.com/hemberg-lab/VASAseq_2022

other.label: Received / accepted / published
other.value: Received 20 Nov 2021; accepted 13 May 2022; published online 27 Jun 2022; Nature Biotechnology 40, 1780-1793 (Dec 2022).

data_processing.summary: VASA-plate reads are demultiplexed by the Read-1 6-nt UFI/UMI + 8-nt well barcode (Hamming ≤1 to the 384-well whitelist); Read 2 is trimmed and homopolymer-clipped, ribo-depleted in silico, aligned with STAR, and molecules are counted per gene by UFI with strand specificity. Downstream analysis covers doublet removal, Scanpy/Leiden clustering, scVelo RNA velocity, and Whippet/MicroExonator alternative-splicing quantification.
data_processing.statistical_model: UFI-based (unique fragment identifier) molecule counting, strand-specific, with Hamming-distance-1 barcode correction — no parametric count model; downstream: two-sided t-test for differential expression, beta-distribution CDF-beta for differential splicing-node inclusion, and a bimodal-vs-single Weibull mixture for histone/cell-cycle classification
